title: Agents and Infrastructure, Sam Lambert | Compile 26
summary: Sam Lambert, CEO of PlanetScale, argues that database infrastructure must change to enable agents to optimize and manage databases autonomously. He demonstrates agents performing database sharding and optimization with safety mechanisms like branching, rollback capabilities, and human-in-the-loop verification.
writeup: PlanetScale CEO Sam Lambert argues that database and systems infrastructure should be redesigned around AI agents rather than human operators. The talk demonstrates agents optimizing database sharding and executing database changes, backed by safety mechanisms including branching and undo capabilities. The central claim: gains in agent capability depend on building suitable infrastructure, not only on more intelligent models.
